story.iWant: a project detail view with tabs for Threads, Files, Members, and Settings
story.soThat: I can manage all project resources in a single organized interface with proper deletion safeguards
story.tasks: - Implement Tabbed Interface (Threads, Files, Members, Settings tabs) - Implement Threads Tab (display project threads, "New Thread" button) - Implement Files Tab (placeholder for Epic 5, show "coming soon" message) - Implement Members Tab (display member list, delegate management to E3-S7) - Implement Settings Tab (project metadata, "Edit" and "Delete" buttons owner-only) - Implement Project Deletion with Safeguards (confirmation dialog, type project name, CASCADE delete, audit logging) - Implement Refresh Button (in detail view toolbar) - Performance and Testing (view load <500ms, E2E cascade delete, audit table verification)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Detail view has tabbed interface: Threads, Files, Members, Settings
Threads tab shows project threads (filtered by projectId)
Files tab shows project files (requires Epic 5 File Service)
Members tab shows member list with roles + pending invitations section
Settings tab shows project metadata (name, description, color from Moku Web palette, icon)
Delete confirmation dialog shows: "This will permanently delete X threads and Y messages. This action cannot be undone."
Delete confirmation requires typing project name to confirm (prevent accidental deletion)
Project deletion CASCADE deletes all threads and messages (ON DELETE CASCADE foreign key)
Deleted threads/messages logged to PostgreSQL audit table (audit trail preserved)
"Refresh" button visible in detail view toolbar

doc.section: 1. Owner clicks "Delete Project" in Settings tab 2. Confirmation dialog shows warning and requires typing project name 3. User types project name exactly (case-sensitive validation) 4. ProjectService.delete() calls Moku API: DELETE /api/projects/{id} 5. Database CASCADE DELETE: ON DELETE CASCADE foreign key deletes threads/messages 6. Audit logging: Deleted threads/messages logged to audit table 7. ProjectService invalidates all project-related caches 8. UI navigates back to project list
ALTER TABLE desktop_threads ADD CONSTRAINT fk_project_id FOREIGN KEY (project_id) REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE;
audit_log: { id, entity_type, entity_id, action, actor_user_id, metadata (projectId, threadIds, messageIds, counts), created_at }
- Delete Project: Owner only - Edit Project: Owner only - Invite Members: Owner only (handled in E3-S7) - View Threads/Files/Members: All roles (owner/editor/viewer)

code.file: Delete confirmation modal component Shows warning, requires typing project name
ProjectService.deleteProject(id, deleteThreads) IPC call to backend
IPC handler for project:delete Handles cascade delete or thread unassignment

constraints.constraint: Delete confirmation requires typing project name exactly
ON DELETE CASCADE foreign key for project_id deletes threads/messages
All deletions logged to PostgreSQL audit table
Files tab placeholder until Epic 5 complete

interface.name: ProjectDetailView
interface.method: Fetches project data and populates tabs
Opens confirmation dialog, deletes on confirm
Invalidates cache and reloads project data

interface.name: IPC
interface.method: Gets thread count for deletion warning

tests.standards: - Unit tests: Tab navigation, permission checks (owner-only delete button) - E2E tests: Full cascade delete flow (create → add threads → delete → verify audit table) - E2E tests: Delete confirmation requires typing project name - Integration tests: Verify ON DELETE CASCADE foreign key, audit table logging - Performance tests: View load time <500ms, tab switch <100ms - Manual tests: Deletion confirmation dialog UX, audit table entries

ideas.idea: Test tabbed interface has all 4 tabs: Threads, Files, Members, Settings
Test Threads tab displays project threads filtered by projectId
Test Files tab shows placeholder message until Epic 5
Test Members tab displays member list with roles
Test Settings tab displays project metadata (name, description, color, icon)
Test "Delete Project" button visible only to owner, hidden for editor/viewer
Test delete confirmation shows thread/message counts
Test confirmation requires typing project name exactly (case-sensitive)
Integration test: CASCADE delete removes all threads and messages
Integration test: Audit table contains deletion entries with metadata
Test "Refresh" button invalidates cache and reloads data
